title: Silky Smooth Polish Nuka Pickle Pasta
description: A rich and fluffy Polish dish featuring dried leaves of summer savory and nuka-pickle, perfect for a midnight craving, this recipe is low in calories and friendly to those watching their weight.
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the whole wheat pasta according to the package instructions until al dente.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat and add the chopped nuka-pickle, cooking until lightly browned.
Add the dried summer savory leaves, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked pasta, nuka-pickle mixture, and low-fat sour cream, stirring until the pasta is well coated.
Stir in the chopped fresh dill and grated low-fat cheddar cheese.
Serve the pasta hot, garnished with additional chopped fresh dill if desired.
Let the pasta cool to room temperature before refrigerating or freezing for later use.
Reheat the pasta in the microwave or on the stovetop over low heat, stirring frequently, until warmed through.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
